threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think there's a 'right' way to navigate the bus routes. Locals just follow the vibe—everyone knows the 243 has more stops than sense.
Heard a guy on the 3 bus argue passionately with his reflection in the window about whether pineapple belongs on pizza. Classic Brixton vibe.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted a group of kids using a broken trampoline as a makeshift stage for their rap battles at the park. Pure Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find a 'silent disco' at an abandoned warehouse, where everyone's dancing with headphones while the city sleeps outside.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a local knows to avoid the Civic Center BART on Fridays—it's like a smoke and mirror show when the brakes overheat right before rush hour.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5 this morning—bus driver stopped to let a guy finish his street performance before the light changed. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's just about the parks, but locals know the real charm lies in the hidden street art and the smells wafting from neighborhood kitchens.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell when someone’s new—they're the ones staring at the metro map like it's a treasure map, trying to figure out how to get to Plaça del Sol.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of just 'the train'—the locals know it's all about the Overground here.
Last night on the 59, a bloke serenaded us with 90s R&B. Whole bus went silent, just vibing. Moments like this make late-night travel worth it.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw the kebab shop owner giving leftover bread to the guy with the guitar. It's little moments like that that make it feel like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a techno party in a former swimming pool, complete with disco balls and the sound of water echoing in the background.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they instinctively know which BART entrance at Civic Center is least likely to be a mess when an emergency hits.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5-Fulton today as a pigeon tried to board. It just stood there, defiant, while the driver waved it away like it was part of the commute.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think they can just hop on a tram to see the sights, but half of them don't know the tram doesn’t even reach the heart of Gràcia.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the two shopping bags they carry: one from El Corte Inglés and another from a local market, confused about where to grab groceries.
